Title: Exploiting genetic diversity and gene synthesis to identify superior nitrogenase NifH protein variants to engineer N2-fixation in plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides fixed nitrogen through enabling nitrogenase maturation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports [Fe-S] cluster assembly/delivery for NifH maturation; in tobacco, Fe fertilization increased soluble NbNifU accumulation and in vitro NifU-mediated [Fe-S] transfer activated NbNifHHt
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with nifH in tobacco and yeast; immunoblotting; purification from tobacco; Fe fertilization response; in vitro NifU-mediated [Fe-S] reconstitution assay activating NbNifHHt
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mitochondrial matrix (SU9-targeted in yeast and tobacco)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A. vinelandii nifU was co-expressed as an accessory maturation factor to support NifH [Fe-S] cluster assembly. 2. Fe fertilization increased soluble NbNifU accumulation in tobacco mitochondria. 3. Cluster-loaded Ec/Nb NifU activity supported activation of tobacco-isolated H. thermophilus NifH, implicating [Fe-S] delivery as limiting in plants.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the main screening target but experimentally tested as part of the maturation bottleneck; authors conclude plant mitochondrial [Fe-S] cluster availability is limiting.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: E35S constitutive promoter in tobacco; GAL1/GAL10 inducible promoters in yeast
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct growth penalty reported; possible instability/truncation in tobacco purification
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NifU scaffold protein for [Fe-S] cluster assembly
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves; induced in yeast mitochondria
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory engineering target to improve NifH maturation and nitrogenase assembly in plants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fe fertilization of soil; dark harvest; aerobic growth of yeast/tobacco with anaerobic purification

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides fixed nitrogen through enabling nitrogenase maturation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports sulfur mobilization for [Fe-S] cluster biosynthesis needed for NifH maturation; co-expression enabled testing of NifH variants but did not itself respond to Fe fertilization in tobacco
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with nifH/nifU in tobacco and yeast; immunoblotting; use of EcNifS in direct [Fe-S] reconstitution assays
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mitochondrial matrix (SU9-targeted in yeast and tobacco)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A. vinelandii nifS was included as an accessory factor for NifH [Fe-S] cluster biosynthesis in tobacco and yeast mitochondria. 2. Unlike NifU, tobacco NifS abundance was not increased by Fe fertilization. 3. NifS activity was used in direct in vitro [Fe-S] reconstitution assays that restored activity of tobacco-isolated NifHHt.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accessory maturation factor rather than primary screening target
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: E35S constitutive promoter in tobacco; GAL1/GAL10 inducible promoters in yeast
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves; induced in yeast mitochondria
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Accessory target for improving [Fe-S] cluster supply in plant nitrogenase engineering
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fe fertilization; dark harvest; aerobic growth with anaerobic purification

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides fixed nitrogen indirectly by assisting nitrogenase Fe protein maturation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Included to support NifH folding/dimerization prior to [Fe-S] acquisition during heterologous expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with nifH variants in tobacco and yeast; immunoblotting for protein accumulation
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mitochondrial matrix (SU9-targeted in yeast and tobacco)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A. vinelandii nifM was co-expressed consistently with all screened nifH variants as a putative NifH maturation factor. 2. It was included even for source organisms lacking nifM homologs, to standardize the screen. 3. Its sufficiency/necessity for the superior H. thermophilus NifH remains unresolved and is noted as future work.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accessory chaperone-like factor; not demonstrated here to be metabolite-determining on its own
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: E35S constitutive promoter in tobacco; GAL1/GAL10 inducible promoters in yeast
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NifH maturation/folding factor; prolyl isomerase-like protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves; induced in yeast mitochondria
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive component for plant nitrogenase assembly, but requirement may be variant-specific

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides biologically useful fixed nitrogen for plant nutrition and reduced fertilizer dependence
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Biological nitrogen fixation / molybdenum nitrogenase pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nitrogenase Fe protein; ATP-dependent reductase; [Fe4S4]-binding P-loop NTPase-like protein
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers electrons from the Fe protein to the MoFe protein (NifDK) in an ATP-dependent manner to support substrate reduction; also supports P-cluster maturation and FeMo-co biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ATP; electrons from reductant; NifDK/NifEN-associated maturation intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced NifDK for N2-to-NH3 conversion; matured P-cluster-containing NifDK; FeMo-co biosynthesis support
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream electron-donor and cofactor-maturation step essential for terminal N2 reduction
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for formation and function of the terminal product
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mitochondrial matrix (when expressed in yeast and tobacco)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves; heterologously expressed in yeast under galactose induction
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: E35S constitutive promoter in tobacco; GAL1/GAL10 inducible promoters in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Aerobic culture; dark-period harvest in tobacco; Fe fertilization; anaerobic purification/assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting enzymatically, but fulfills quantity requirements and [Fe-S] cluster loading is a bottleneck for engineering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 32-variant tobacco mitochondrial screen; soluble/total immunoblotting; yeast mitochondrial expression and Strep-tag purification; acetylene reduction assay; N2 reduction assay; EPR and UV-vis; in vivo complementation of A. vinelandii ΔnifH; in vitro P-cluster maturation and FeMo-co synthesis assays; [Fe-S] reconstitution assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: H. thermophilus nifH produced a highly soluble and functional NifH in yeast and tobacco mitochondria, outperforming A. vinelandii nifH for accumulation; in tobacco, activity was limited by poor [Fe-S] cluster loading but could be strongly increased by in vitro reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower specific activity with A. vinelandii NifDK than native A. vinelandii NifH; tobacco-expressed protein largely apo due to limited mitochondrial [Fe-S] cluster availability/stability
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value donor nifH for plant nitrogenase engineering; should be combined with improved mitochondrial [Fe-S] cluster assembly/protection and possibly additional accessory factors
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NifDK; NifEN; NifU; NifS; NifM; NifX; NifB
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the superior natural NifH variant among 32 phylogenetically diverse nifH genes screened. Soluble accumulation in yeast mitochondria was ~20-fold higher than A. vinelandii NifH and translated to at least 10-fold higher in vivo activity potential. In tobacco, as-isolated protein was mostly inactive until [Fe4S4] reconstitution, revealing plant mitochondrial [Fe-S] cluster availability as a major bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among 32 nifH variants, Hydrogenobacter thermophilus nifH was one of the only variants consistently accumulating as soluble protein in tobacco mitochondria and was the best functional candidate. 2. Yeast mitochondria-expressed H. thermophilus NifH was purified at high yield, carried an [Fe4S4] cluster, supported acetylene and N2 reduction with A. vinelandii NifDK, and functioned in P-cluster maturation and FeMo-co synthesis. 3. In tobacco mitochondria, the same NifH accumulated well but was largely inactive until in vitro [Fe4S4] reconstitution, demonstrating that mitochondrial [Fe-S] cluster supply/stability is a central engineering bottleneck.
